title: TMC moves SC to scrap EC order excluding State staff from vote counting supervisor duty
content: The high court also said while dismissing the petition, “It is the prerogative of the office of the Election Commission of India to appoint the counting supervisor and counting assistant either from the state government or the central government. This court does not find any illegality for appointing counting supervisor and counting assistant from the central government / central PSU employee instead of state government employee.”

title: Middle East conflict drags on: Is Iran’s economy approaching breaking point?
content: Weeks of conflict have intensified Iran's economic pressures, straining its financial system despite ongoing internal resilience and limited external trade. While facing significant business challenges and rising costs, Iran is leveraging domestic resources and alternative trade routes to sustain itself amidst a Gulf standoff and stalled ceasefire talks.

title: NSA is testing Anthropic’s Mythos to find security flaws in Microsoft products
content: The NSA is testing Anthropic's advanced AI model, Mythos, to identify cybersecurity vulnerabilities in Microsoft products. This initiative highlights the agency's growing interest in AI-driven security testing, with officials impressed by the model's speed and efficiency in detecting weaknesses. The move comes amid broader discussions about the controlled deployment of powerful AI systems in sensitive government domains.

title: Anti-climax in Jaipur! Starc vs Sooryavanshi fizzles out as Jamieson steals the show
content: Kyle Jamieson stole the spotlight in the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Rajasthan Royals, dismissing teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early.  Mitchell Starc also took a wicket on his season debut, but Jamieson's crucial strike prevented the anticipated duel with Sooryavanshi from materialising, giving DC early control.

title: Super El-Nino, drought & heat hell: India staring at longest, hottest summer yet
content: India is experiencing extreme heatwaves, with temperatures soaring and heatwaves arriving earlier and with greater intensity. This is exacerbated by the El Niño phenomenon, which is expected to strengthen. The heat poses significant health risks and economic challenges, impacting daily life and livelihoods across the nation. Adaptation and mitigation strategies are crucial for navigating this growing crisis.

title: CEO says ‘When Google CEO agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
content: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: Medal assured! India crush Chinese Taipei 3-0 to storm into Thomas Cup semis
content: India secured a commanding 3-0 victory over Chinese Taipei to storm into the Thomas Cup semifinals, guaranteeing at least a bronze medal. Lakshya Sen ignited the charge with a comeback win, followed by a dominant doubles performance from Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty. Ayush Shetty sealed the tie with a composed straight-game victory.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 44: Ranveer Singh film eyes 9-year global record of 'Baahubali 2'
content: ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ is rewriting the record books as it nears the end of its seventh week. Having already become the first Hindi film to join the Rs. 1,000 crore net club in India, the film’s total domestic net now stands at a staggering Rs. 1,135.25 crore. On Day 44 of its theatrical run, live tracking shows the film maintaining a steady grip on the audience despite a reduced screen count. With a current worldwide gross of Rs. 1,783.90 crore, the Ranveer Singh-starrer is just steps away from overtaking the lifetime global collections of Baahubali 2.

title: OpenAI addresses 'ChatGPT obsession' with Goblins and Gremlins, AI Agents
content: ChatGPT's recent unusual references to goblins and other creatures stemmed from a 'Nerdy' personality setting in GPT-5.1, which inadvertently rewarded such language. OpenAI identified this pattern, where these mentions were disproportionately high in the 'Nerdy' mode. The company has since removed the problematic setting and adjusted training to prevent recurrence.

title: R Madhavan’s weight loss secrets: Fasting, diet, early dinner at 55
content: R Madhavan, still a heartthrob at 55, attributes his enduring appeal and fitness to a strict vegetarian diet and a disciplined lifestyle.  He recently shared his weight loss secrets, emphasizing intermittent fasting, mindful eating, early dinners, and prioritizing sleep and hydration.  Fans eagerly await his reunion with Kangana Ranaut.

title: 'All speculation put to rest': Mann's trust motion passes Punjab assembly, takes jab at Cong
content: The Aam Aadmi Party government in Punjab moved a confidence motion in the state assembly following the defection of seven MPs to the BJP. Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann asserted the party's strength and national presence, aiming to dispel rumors of instability. AAP holds a significant majority in the 117-member assembly.

title: Maria Sharapova net worth 2026: $200M fortune from tennis & business
content: Maria Sharapova, a tennis icon, amassed an estimated $180 million net worth primarily through lucrative endorsement deals and her candy brand, Sugarpova. Her remarkable career included winning all four Grand Slams and reaching world No. 1. After retiring in 2020, she continues to thrive in business and investments, maintaining her global recognition.

title: Exclusive: Sargam Ki Sadhe Satii actor Akash Makhija ties the knot with childhood sweetheart Roshni
content: Acclaimed actor Akash Makhija, recognized for his remarkable role in 'Sargam Ki Sadhe Satii', celebrated a heartfelt wedding with his longtime love, Roshni Budhiraja, on April 25, 2026, in the vibrant city of Delhi. Their ceremony, beautifully curated by the Sant Nirankari Mission, radiated an essence of simplicity and unity.

title: ‘Dhurandhar’ singer Vaibhav Gupta praises Aditya Dhar’s music sense
content: Singer Vaibhav has revealed in an interview how composer Shashwat Sachdev discovered him through an Instagram reel and brought him to the attention of director Aditya Dhar for a song in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'. Vaibhav Gupta described his meeting at Aditya Dhar's home as a revelation, sharing that the director plays the sitar and offered specific, technically informed guidance on vocal modulation throughout the recording process. Since the film's release on March 19, 2026, music directors across the industry have sent Vaibhav Gupta personal messages saying they "haven't heard a voice like his in a long time." Previously seen on Indian Idol, Vaibhav credits the 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' experience as transformative both professionally and personally.

title: ‘Never dreamt of this’: KL Rahul stunned by Sooryavanshi’s audacious hitting
content: KL Rahul lauded Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, highlighting his phenomenal rise as a testament to T20 cricket's evolution. Sooryavanshi's fearless approach and exceptional batting, including two centuries at a young age, showcase the new generation's aggressive mindset, a trend amplified by the IPL.

title: 'This is election bill': Rahul flays BJP over Rs 993 LPG price jump, warns petrol & diesel next
content: Rahul Gandhi has sharply criticized the Centre over rising inflation, highlighting a significant Rs 993 jump in commercial LPG cylinder prices in a single day. He warned that this surge, amounting to an 81% increase over three months, will heavily impact small businesses and household expenses, predicting further hikes in petrol and diesel prices.

title: Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal may spark more bombshells
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s controversy may not be over, as analysts warn more details could emerge. While Russini has stepped away and Vrabel has returned to the New England Patriots, speculation continues. Analyst Kelvin Washington suggested new information could surface as more sources come forward. Despite this, players like Drake Maye have shown support for Vrabel.

title: ‘He won’t be Bumrah’: Bishop questions Sooryavanshi’s first-ball six chances vs Starc
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has impressed in IPL 2026, scoring 400 runs at a strike rate of 238.10 against top bowlers. He now faces a new challenge against Mitchell Starc, who has recovered from injury and joined Delhi Capitals.  The duel between the fearless youngster and the experienced Australian quick is anticipated to be a key contest.

title: HC rejects plea seeking FIR against Rahul over 'fighting the Indian State' remark
content: The Allahabad high court has dismissed a plea demanding an FIR against Rahul Gandhi for allegedly calling the "Indian State" an opponent. A petitioner claimed Gandhi's remark during an AICC office inauguration in 2025 was seditious and anti-national, hurting public sentiment. Justice Vikram D Chauhan rejected the challenge to a lower court's decision.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi poised for yet another record, one big hit away from 100 sixes
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is on the verge of a major T20 milestone, needing just one six to reach 100 career sixes. He has already achieved several remarkable records this IPL season, including the fastest 400 runs and youngest to 1,000 T20 runs.

title: Watch: Supriya Sule stuck in massive Mumbai–Pune Expressway jam, misses Maharashtra Day event
content: NCP MP Supriya Sule faced significant traffic jams while traveling to Pune. She was stuck for nearly two hours, highlighting the daily struggles of commuters. Earlier, she experienced a long wait for a cab in Mumbai, facing refusals and limited availability. These incidents underscore persistent issues with last-mile connectivity and ride availability in the city.

title: Dianna Russini scandal: NFL urged to probe A.J. Brown
content: Pablo Torre has urged the NFL to review Dianna Russini’s role in linking A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots. While the league views the Russini-Mike Vrabel situation as personal, Torre raised concerns about optics and potential scrutiny. The NFL has no plans to investigate, but attention could grow if trade talks involving Brown move forward.

title: Gill’s ‘Play Hold’ Dig: GT captain mocks RCB slogan after win; rivalry intensifies online
content: Shubman Gill's cryptic Instagram post, playing on RCB's slogan "Play Bold" after Gujarat Titans' victory, quickly went viral. GT secured a four-wicket win, avenging an earlier defeat and continuing their late-season surge. The win was marked by a disciplined bowling effort and crucial batting performances.

title: More work, less security: The hidden divide in India's job story
content: While India's workforce participation has increased, a deeper look reveals significant disparities. More people are working, but many are in self-employment or informal roles with uncertain incomes. Gender and location heavily influence access to stable, well-paying jobs, highlighting a critical imbalance in job quality and opportunity.

title: Indian student dies by suicide in Chicago, leaves scheduled messages for friends
content: An Indian-origin student from Andhra Pradesh, Chandu Iraganaboyina, studying in Chicago, has allegedly died by suicide. Police are investigating how he obtained a firearm on a non-immigrant visa, with friends and family citing severe financial stress as a potential cause. The Telugu community in the US has initiated a fundraiser to repatriate his body.

title: 'Life jackets given after boat started sinking': What led to Jabalpur tragedy that killed 9
content: A cruise boat operated by the Madhya Pradesh tourism department capsized in Bargi Dam near Jabalpur, resulting in at least nine deaths. The vessel, carrying 43 passengers, overturned during a sudden, severe storm with high winds. Survivors allege life jackets were distributed only after the boat began sinking, raising questions about safety protocols.

title: Virat Kohli ignores fan’s autograph request; child’s reaction teaches parents a lesson
content: A viral clip shows a young fan crying after Virat Kohli walks past without signing his bat. The incident sparked online debate about celebrity interaction, childhood disappointment, and emotional regulation. The moment highlights the challenges parents face in teaching resilience through everyday let-downs.

title: 'God wanted Mojtaba preserved': Iran calls US claims on Supreme Leader 'trick used by enemy'
content: An Iranian official dismissed U.S. claims about Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's health, stating he is in perfect health and actively managing state affairs. The official confirmed Khamenei survived a bombing, sustaining injuries but being preserved by divine will. This contradicts U.S. media reports alleging critical medical challenges and limited engagement following the attack.

title: Watch: J&K CM Omar Abdullah shares video of bears ‘stealing’ flag at Srinagar golf course
content: An unusual sight unfolded at Srinagar's Royal Spring Golf Course. Two bears entered the premises and made off with a flag. Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah shared a video of the incident online. The bears' playful act amused onlookers. The golf course's proximity to forests often leads to wildlife sightings.

title: Holder catch controversy: 'Sufficient evidence for not out' - Bishop on Patidar wicket
content: Former cricketer Ian Bishop believes Rajat Patidar should have been ruled not out after Jason Holder's controversial catch. Bishop cited insufficient control and doubt about the ball touching the ground, suggesting the umpire should have reviewed more closely. Patidar's dismissal significantly impacted RCB's innings.

title: Top Iranian leaders push to remove FM Abbas Araghchi over alleged allegiance to IRGC
content: For the first time in 23 years, the U.S. has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East, accompanied by 12 ships and 15,000 soldiers. This significant military presence signals readiness for renewed conflict should the fragile ceasefire with Iran collapse. The deployment occurs amid ongoing diplomatic tensions and Iran's reblocking of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Fire Services Dept. gears up to tackle rising summer heat
content: With the number of ‘fire calls’ increasing, 2,386 in April alone, field staff have been advised to stay alert and leave is not being sanctioned unless urgently required, says Fire Services in-charge Director General Venkata Ramana

title: Siddipet court imposes fines worth ₹3.34 lakh on 33 motorists for drunk driving
content: In a separate drive, seven individuals were fined a total of ₹35,000 for driving without valid licences.

title: Harish Rao alleges ₹5,000 cr. scam in HAM roads’ tenders
content: He says the government has handed over work tenders to those who quoted 25% in excess of estimated cost

title: Elephant brought to temple turns violent, kills one, injures mahout
content: An elephant flipped over a car several times, destroying it, and also damaged a few two-wheelers present there, even as an Elephant Squad of the Forest Department tried to capture the jumbo before it moved out from the temple premises to a more populated area, says police

title: Tirupati police roll out four-layer plan to make district ganja-free
content: Ganja testing kits introduced as part of strategy to identify offenders and curb narcotics menace, says SP

title: GBA to launch weekly ‘e-Khata open houses’ across 50 centres in Bengaluru
content: The initiative will begin after May 15. At each centre, around 100 citizens will be invited in advance, with their cases pre-screened and examined by officials prior to the meeting.

title: Hike in commercial LPG price: Shock, resignation on food street as LPG price goes up by 42%
content: Hyderabad food kiosk and restaurant owners express shock over the sharp ₹994 hike in commercial LPG cylinder prices, fearing higher costs and fresh pressure on menu rates

title: How hike in cylinder price will affect food prices
content: Beverages are set to get costlier by about 25%, while food items are likely to rise by at least 20% across small and mid-scale eateries, according to hoteliers

title: Kurnool student’s alleged suicide in Chicago: Lokesh asks Union Aviation Minister to expedite repatriation
content: The Andhra Pradesh HRD Minister assured the family of swift assistance after a TDP IT cell member flagged the case on social media; the Kurnool district police said no formal communication had been received

title: Sustainable healthcare practices key to future, says ICMR expert
content: Doctors’ role critical in helping the country achieve its vision of becoming a developed nation by 2047, says the director of the National Institute for Research in Environmental Health at the graduation day of Kodagu Institute of Medical Sciences in Madikeri

title: More flight cuts loom at Air India amid cost pressures
content: While airline sources said domestic flights have not been cut, the CEO indicated they are also under profitability pressure

title: Noida International airport to start commercial flights from June 15
content: On March 28, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi formally inaugurated the National Capital Region’s second airport, the Noida International Airport

title: Jagan peddling false numbers on construction of Amaravati, says Minority Affairs Adviser Shariff
content: While the government is spending an average of ₹6,980 per square foot, the YSRCP chief is misleading the public by inflating the number to ₹12,000 to ₹14,000 per square feet, he alleges

title: A.P. govt. will set up ‘Labour Addas’ to upskill workers, says CM Naidu
content: 15 Labour Addas to be set up in the first phase; CM distributes ₹5 lakh each to 35 construction workers  and presents Shrama Shakti awards to workers and industrial managements, marking the May Day

title: LNG ship from India heading towards Hormuz drops anchor
content: Umm Al Ashtan had stayed close to Oman during its transit unlike typical Iran-approved transits that hug the Iranian coast.

title: Under-performing MLAs will have no second chance, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: Asserting that Andhra Pradesh is witnessing a renewed phase of development under NDA dispensation, the Chief Minister exhorts people to reject destructive forces as the State’s future depends on decisive leadership and sustained development

title: NCERT printing Class 9 books on war-footing; supply being normalised
content: To ensure wider access, the NCERT said its textbooks are being made available through multiple channels, including a nationwide vendor network and sales counters at its regional centres

title: IFS officers shuffled in Andhra Pradesh
content: APFDC Chief General Manager Revathi has been transferred and posted as Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Vigilance & Production)

title: AAP govt. wins confidence motion in Punjab Assembly
content: Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann said the AAP was united, but the motion was to counter “false rumours and news”

title: ED empowered under Modi govt. to fight ‘cancer’ of financial crimes, not target anyone: MoS Finance
content: Pankaj Chaudhary, while addressing the 70th Enforcement Day event, said the Enforcement Directorate has been strengthened to effectively tackle the threats of black money, corporate frauds, terror financing, and much more

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: High drama in Punjab Assembly; Congress, Akali Dal allege CM Mann was drunk during House proceedings
content: Congress MLAs staged a walkout and urged the Speaker to initiate action, while the party also called for dope testing of all MLAs and an independent medical check by PGIMER

title: How Tamil Nadu waited for Assembly election results over the decades
content: For the first time in Tamil Nadu’s history, the Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were deployed in all the 234 Assembly segments in the 2001 Assembly elections, mirroring simultaneous rollouts in Kerala, Puducherry, and West Bengal

title: Hit hard by ₹991 price hike for commercial LPG, Mysuru hoteliers say rate went up by ₹1,440 in two months
content: Describing the surge as ‘abnormal and alarming’, the Mysuru Hotel Owners’ Association said the sharp rise over just two months is placing mounting pressure on the hospitality industry and small businesses

title: 6 Of Family Killed As Speeding Car Rams Parked Truck In Hyderabad
content: Hyderabad, May 1 (IANS) Six members of a family were killed when a car in which they were travelling rammed into a parked truck on Outer Ring Road (ORR) on the outskirts of Hyderabad on Friday.

title: Kolkata EVM Strongrooms Fortified After Mamata Banerjee's Visit, Trinamool's Protests
content: The Kolkata Police's move follows high drama last night due to the Trinamool Congress's allegations of suspicious activities at counting centres

title: 'Concerned': Taiwan After China Calls Taipei Biggest Risk In Ties With US
content: In a call with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio Thursday, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi urged United States to "make the right choices" on Taiwan.

title: 'Don't Accept Imposition, Don't Want War': Iran Says Open To Talk To US
content: Iran and the United States held a single round of talks against the backdrop of a fragile ceasefire following nearly 40 days of war.

title: Iran Crisis Making Delivery Of Relief Aid To Refugees Costly : UN Agency
content: According to UNHCR spokesperson Carlotta Wolf, people in dire need are receiving things that are ready later than when needed,

title: Operation Global Hunt: An India-Interpol Plan To Track Down Fugitives
content: The targets of this operation include criminals linked to international drug syndicates and organised crime networks.

title: History Of RSS' And 'Modi Tatva' A Must For MSU Baroda Sociology Students
content: The decision to include the topics stems from a perceived need to bridge the gap between academic research and practical governance

title: Trump To Venture Out Of White House For 1st Time Since Shooting Horror
content: Trump will travel to Florida on Friday, a stronghold under mounting political pressure and intense scrutiny of his security.

title: Oscar Statuette For 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin' Goes Missing On Flight
content: Talankin, who fled Russia in 2024, has defended the film as a record for posterity to show how "an entire generation became angry and aggressive".

title: Top Five Moments Of King Charles III With Trump During US Visit
content: While Charles's visit to the US is hailed as a "masterclass in effective diplomacy", its impact on Trump's and Starmer's relations is yet to be seen.

title: 'Treacherous Aggression': UAE Says Iran Cannot Be Trusted Over Hormuz
content: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei cautioned on Thursday against expecting quick results from talks.

title: "Strategic Necessity, Not Destruction": Centre On Great Nicobar Project
content: Officials said the island's proximity of about 40 nautical miles to one of the world's busiest East-West shipping routes gives it a natural advantage for global trade

title: Conviction Rate At 94% Despite Legal Challenges, Says Probe Agency Chief
content: He added, "Today, the criminal landscape is defined by cryptocurrency fraud, cyber-enabled financial crimes, terror financing, anti-national activities, and narcotics trafficking."

title: Learn Marathi If You Live In Maharashtra, But No Violence: D Fadnavis
content: Mumbai, May 1 (IANS) Amid escalating debate over the mandatory use of the Marathi language in Maharashtra, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis on Friday emphasised that while pride in one's mother tongue is essential, the state government will not toler

title: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 993 Amid War, Petrol, Diesel Rates Steady
content: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 993 Amid Iran-US War, No Changes In Domestic Rates, Petrol, Diesel Prices

title: A Tale Of Two Countries: France's Nuclear Bet vs Germany's Renewable Push
content: France's decades-long investment in nuclear power now appears to be a strategic advantage. In Germany, fossil imports still play a central role.

title: US Eyeing $15 Million 'Dark Eagle' Hypersonic Strike Plan To Counter Iran
content: The objective behind the proposal is to enhance the US military's ability to strike Iranian ballistic missile launchers located deep inside the country.

title: Punjab CM 'drunk' in assembly? LoP demands alco-meter testing of Mann, AAP MLAs
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
